INTRO_IDENTIFY.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, and verify the patient's identity using two identifiers (name and date of birth, or wristband ID).
INTRO_IDENTIFY.clinicalNote: When: Skills where the wrong identity = real harm (feeding S10, catheter S17, bed bath S11). How: "Hi, I'm [name], your nursing assistant. May I check your ID band?"

INTRO_EXPLAIN.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
INTRO_EXPLAIN.clinicalNote: Respirations only: Say "I'll be timing your breathing" — never say "counting"; awareness changes the rate. On exam day: Introduce once at the start; GWC does not require re-introduction for each skill REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf.

PRIVACY.detailedText: Provide for patient privacy — close the curtain, door, or privacy screen.
PRIVACY.clinicalNote: Do it: any time the body is exposed (bath, PROM, transfers, peri-care, dressing, stocking). Skip it: pulse, BP, weight, urinary output, hand hygiene, dentures — no body exposure ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md.

HAND_HYGIENE.detailedText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
HAND_HYGIENE.clinicalNote: Always physically demonstrate the first wash of your exam session — this is your only full demo. GWC Key Step = automatic fail if skipped REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Phrase: "Give me a moment — I'm going to wash my hands."

HAND_HYGIENE.detailedText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
HAND_HYGIENE.clinicalNote: Verbalize only after the first wash: "At this time I would perform hand hygiene." Occurs mid-skill when switching from dirty to clean tasks (e.g., bedpan S04, peri-care S21).

HAND_HYGIENE.detailedText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
HAND_HYGIENE.clinicalNote: Verbalize: "At this time I would wash my hands." Michelle's exact phrase in S07, S11, S12 ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Washing before documenting satisfies GWC's before-recording requirement.

WATER_CHECK.detailedText: Fill the basin with warm water. Verify the temperature is safe and comfortable — approximately 105–110°F (40–43°C) — using a thermometer, or by having the patient test with their hand or a wet washcloth on the back of their hand.
WATER_CHECK.clinicalNote: Dual-check: CNA tests first (wrist or inner elbow), then patient confirms comfort. S14 transcript: "Mr. Smith, can you check the water temperature for me?" ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Range: 105–110°F.

WATER_CHECK.detailedText: Fill the basin with warm water. Verify the temperature is safe and comfortable — approximately 105–110°F (40–43°C) — using a thermometer, or by having the patient test with their hand or a wet washcloth on the back of their hand.
WATER_CHECK.clinicalNote: Water cools during extended soaks (foot care: 5–20 min). Recheck before prolonged immersion. Same dual-check: you test, patient confirms.

GLOVE_DON.clinicalNote: GWC rule: Gloves required before any contact with body fluids, mucous membranes, or non-intact skin. Failure to glove = no credit; cannot be corrected mid-skill REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. In PPE (S02): pull cuff over gown sleeve to cover skin.

GLOVE_REMOVE.detailedText: Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface.
GLOVE_REMOVE.clinicalNote: S02 cue: "Dirty to dirty, clean to clean." Never touch the outer surface with bare skin. Order-dependent — cannot be corrected if done wrong REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf.
subSteps: Grasp outside of first glove at wrist, peel off
bare fingers slide under cuff of second glove, peel off inside-out

GLOVE_REMOVE_THEN_HH.detailedText: Remove gloves by turning them inside out and dispose without touching the outer surface, then immediately perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or alcohol-based hand rub if hands are visibly clean.
GLOVE_REMOVE_THEN_HH.clinicalNote: One uninterrupted action — no gap between removal and washing. Used mid-skill in S20 (bedpan) before temporarily leaving the patient. Verbalize the wash after the first demo.

CALL_LIGHT.detailedText: Place the call light or signaling device within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
CALL_LIGHT.clinicalNote: Exam rule: GWC states call signal must always be within reach when leaving REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Order confirmed by transcripts: CALL_LIGHT → BED_LOW → HAND_HYGIENE (S03, S05) ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Say: "Here's your call light — let me know if you need anything."

CALL_LIGHT.detailedText: Place the call light or signaling device within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
CALL_LIGHT.clinicalNote: Silent placement — just put it within reach before stepping away (S20, bedpan step 12). Don't announce it. A second CALL_LIGHT at skill close is still required.

BED_LOW.detailedText: Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
BED_LOW.clinicalNote: Two actions, one step: lower AND lock. Physically press the brake and confirm resistance — evaluator watches for the physical check REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. S03 transcript: "make sure the bed is in low position" before washing ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md.
